title: Repository Baseline, Go 1.26 Toolchain, Modules, and Reproduction · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
Record Go 1.26.5, GOOS, GOARCH, GOTOOLCHAIN, module mode, build tags, and supported platform matrix.
Choose module path, command and internal package boundaries, dependency direction, and import visibility before growth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Repository State
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 9365 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go toolchain, module, clean-build, target, and dependency evidence; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
